en.extract:
The Kafa white-eye (Zosterops kaffensis) is a small passerine bird in the white-eye family, Zosteropidae. It is found in west and southwest Ethiopia as well as on Mount Kulal in north Kenya. It was formerly considered to be a subspecies of Ethiopian white-eye.


Taxonomy
The Kafa white-eye was formally described in 1902 by the German ornithologist Oscar Neumann from a specimen collected near Anderaccha in the Kaffa region of southwest Ethiopia. He placed the new species in the genus Zosterops and coined the current binomial name Zosterops kaffensis. The genus name combines the Ancient Greek zōstēros meaning "belt" or "girdle" with ōpos meaning "eye". The specific epithet kaffensis is from Kaffa Province, a former kingdom in south-western Ethiopia. The Kafa white-eye was formerly considered to be a subspecies of the Ethiopian white-eye. It was elevated to species status based on the divergence of the mitochondrial DNA sequence and differences in plumage.
Two subspecies are recognised:

Z. k. kaffensis Neumann, 1902 – west, southwest Ethiopia
Z. k. kulalensis Williams, JG, 1948 – Mount Kulal (north Kenya)

The '''Kafa white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops kaffensis''''') is a small [[passerine]] bird in the white-eye [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found in west and southwest Ethiopia as well as on [[Mount Kulal]] in north Kenya. It was formerly considered to be a [[subspecies]] of [[Ethiopian white-eye]].

==Taxonomy==
The Kafa white-eye was [[species description|formally described]] in 1902 by the German ornithologist [[Oscar Neumann]] from a specimen collected near [[Anderaccha]] in the Kaffa region of southwest Ethiopia. He placed the new species in the genus ''[[Zosterops]]'' and coined the current [[binomial name]] ''Zosterops kaffensis''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Neumann | first=Oscar | author-link=Oscar Neumann | year=1902 | title=Neues aus Afrika | journal=Ornithologische Monatsberichte | volume=10  | issue=1 | pages=8-10 [10] | language=German | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/32583783 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=332 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482449 }}</ref> The genus name combines the [[Ancient Greek]] ''zōstēros'' meaning "belt" or "girdle" with ''ōpos'' meaning "eye".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=Zosterops | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=Zosterops | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=8 September 2024 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''kaffensis'' is from [[Kaffa Province]], a former [[Kingdom of Kaffa|kingdom]] in south-western Ethiopia.<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=kaffensis | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=kaffensis | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=8 September 2024 }}</ref> The Kafa white-eye was formerly considered to be a [[subspecies]] of the [[Ethiopian white-eye]]. It was elevated to species status based on the [[Genetic divergence|divergence]] of the [[mitochondrial DNA]] sequence and differences in plumage.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Sylviid babblers, parrotbills, white-eyes | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sylvias/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=8 September 2024 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Habel | first1=J.C. | last2=Borghesio | first2=L. | last3=Newmark | first3=W.D. | last4=Day | first4=J.J. | last5=Lens | first5=L. | last6=Husemann | first6=M. | last7=Ulrich | first7=W. | date=2015 | title=Evolution along the Great Rift Valley: phenotypic and genetic differentiation of East African white-eyes (Aves, Zosteropidae) | journal=Ecology and Evolution | volume=5 | issue=21 | pages=4849-4862 | doi=10.1002/ece3.1735 | doi-access=free | pmc=4662327 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Martins | first1=F.C. | last2=Cox | first2=S.C. | last3=Irestedt | first3=M. | last4=Prŷs-Jones | first4=R.P. | last5=Day | first5=J.J. | date=2020 | title=A comprehensive molecular phylogeny of Afrotropical white-eyes (Aves: Zosteropidae) highlights prior underestimation of mainland diversity and complex colonisation history | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=149 | article-number=106843 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2020.106843}}</ref>

Two subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''Z. k. kaffensis'' [[Oscar Neumann|Neumann]], 1902 – west, southwest Ethiopia
* ''Z. k. kulalensis'' [[John G. Williams (ornithologist)|Williams, JG]], 1948 – [[Mount Kulal]] (north Kenya)
